metadata.title: 14. Photosynthesis, Respiration and Nutrition in Plants

questions.question: In which of the following plants, you can find Leghemoglobin?
questions.explanation: Legume nodules possess special protein called LEGHEMOGLOBIN. The synthesis of leghemoglobin is the result of symbiosis because neither bacteria alone nor legume plant alone possess the protein

questions.question: In which among the following categories, Nepenthes khasiana or Indian Pitcher Plant can be placed?
questions.explanation: Nutrition is plants is classified into two main categories: autotrophic and heterotrophic. Heterotrophic plants are further classified into saprophytes, parasite and insectivores. Saprophytes are those plants which grow and live on dead organic matter including animal and plant remains Insectivorous plants are autotrophic but develop adaptations to trap insects in order to supplement the deficiency of Nitrogen in the soil. They feed on insects. They are generally found in nitrogen deficient habitats and hence to compensate the loss, they use insects as a source of nitrogen.

questions.question: Chlorophyll absorbs which of the wavelength of the Sun light ?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs red and blue light but reflects green light.

questions.question: Which of the following is a micronutrient that helps in nitrogen metabolism of plants?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen metabolism is not only one of the basic processes of plant physiology, but also one of the important parts of global chemical cycle. The molybdenum nitrogenase is responsible for most biological nitrogen fixation.

questions.question: The pitcher plant is characterized by which of the following plants ?
questions.explanation: The pitcher plant is characterized as insectivorous. Pitcher plants are several different carnivorous plants. They have modified leaves known as pitfall traps which is a prey-trapping mechanism featuring a deep cavity filled with digestive liquid.
